corpus.Employment Agreement:
EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT

This agreement outlines the terms of employment between the Company and Employee.

1. POSITION AND DUTIES
Employee will serve as [Position] and report to [Manager].
Key responsibilities include project delivery, team collaboration, and client communication.

2. COMPENSATION
Base salary: $120,000 annually, paid bi-weekly.
Bonus: Up to 20% based on annual performance review.

3. BENEFITS
Health insurance (medical, dental, vision) after 30 days.
401(k) matching up to 4% of salary.
Paid time off: 20 days vacation + 10 sick days per year.

4. TERMINATION
At-will employment. Either party may terminate with 2 weeks written notice.
Severance: 2 weeks pay for every year of service (max 12 weeks).
corpus.Remote Work Policy:
REMOTE WORK POLICY

1. ELIGIBILITY
Full-time employees whose roles permit remote work may request approval.
Remote work is a privilege subject to business needs and manager approval.

2. EQUIPMENT & ALLOWANCE
Company provides laptop and monitor.
$500 annual home office stipend for desk, chair, lighting.
Internet reimbursement: up to $50/month for employees without company broadband.

3. SCHEDULE & COMMUNICATION
Core hours: 10 AM - 3 PM local time, Monday-Friday.
Minimum 2 days in office per month for team meetings and collaboration.
Use company communication tools (Slack, Teams) during business hours.

4. SECURITY & DATA PROTECTION
VPN required for all work access.
No public WiFi for work activities.
All devices must be encrypted and password-protected.
corpus.Confidentiality Agreement:
CONFIDENTIALITY & NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

1. CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION
Includes trade secrets, business plans, client lists, financial data, and proprietary technology.
Excludes information that is publicly available or independently developed.

2. OBLIGATIONS
Employee agrees not to disclose confidential information during or after employment.
Must use reasonable care to protect confidential materials.
Secure all documents and reports with passwords or locks.

3. DURATION
Confidentiality obligations continue for 2 years after employment termination.
Trade secret protections remain indefinitely under trade secret law.

4. REMEDIES
Breach may result in legal action and injunctive relief.
Employee acknowledges monetary damages may be inadequate remedy.
corpus.Code of Conduct:
CODE OF CONDUCT & WORKPLACE STANDARDS

1. PROFESSIONAL BEHAVIOR
Treat all colleagues with respect and professionalism.
Maintain appropriate workplace language and communication.
Zero tolerance for harassment, discrimination, or bullying.

2. CONFLICT OF INTEREST
Disclose any conflicts of interest to HR immediately.
Do not work for competitors without written approval.
Board positions and consulting work require executive sign-off.

3. CONFIDENTIALITY & IP
Protect company trade secrets and proprietary information.
Do not share customer data or pricing without authorization.
All work created during employment belongs to the company.

4. POLICY VIOLATIONS & CONSEQUENCES
Violations reported to HR or ethics hotline.
Retaliation against reporters strictly prohibited.
Disciplinary action: warning, probation, suspension, or termination.
corpus.Contractor Agreement:
INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AGREEMENT

1. CLASSIFICATION
Contractor is an independent contractor, not an employee.
No employment relationship, benefits, or tax withholding.
Contractor responsible for own taxes, insurance, and compliance.

2. SCOPE OF WORK
Deliverables: [Specific scope of work]
Timeline: [Project start and end dates]
Payment: $[Amount] upon completion and acceptance.

3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
All work product created is owned by the Company.
Contractor retains rights to pre-existing intellectual property.
Company may use deliverables for any purpose.

4. LIABILITY & INSURANCE
Contractor maintains professional liability insurance.
Company not responsible for contractor's equipment or injuries.
Either party may terminate with 5 business days written notice.
corpus.Paid Time Off:
PAID TIME OFF POLICY

1. ANNUAL ALLOWANCE
Years 0-2: 15 days vacation + 10 sick days
Years 3-5: 20 days vacation + 10 sick days
Years 5+: 25 days vacation + 10 sick days

2. REQUEST PROCESS
Submit requests 2 weeks in advance
Use company time-off system
Manager approval required

3. CARRYOVER
Maximum 5 days carryover to next year
Use it or lose it after Dec 31
Exceptions require executive approval

4. PAYOUT
Unused vacation paid out at termination
Sick days not paid out
